product.name: pi-zig-bun
product.tagline: Next-gen AI coding agent: Zig for performance search, Bun for the agent logic.
product.problem_statement: The existing pi-zig (pure Zig) project is missing a rich runtime for extensibility and hasn't fully optimized its search subsystem. We need a high-performance agent that combines the speed of Zig indexing with the ecosystem and convenience of Bun's runtime for tool execution and agent orchestration.
goals: Merge Zig's performance for search/indexing/TUI with Bun's runtime for the agent loop and extensions.
Achieve fff.nvim-class search: <10ms warmed search at 50k files with typo-resistant fuzzy matching.
Support full TypeScript extensions (skills) natively via Bun.
Maintain compatibility with pi's JSONL session tree format.
Deliver a security-first architecture with capability-based gating for tools.
non_goals: Replacing all Zig components with Bun (we keep Zig for the performance-critical parts).
Building a full-blown IDE (pi-zig-bun is a CLI-first tool).

v1_must_have: Zig-powered search engine with fff.nvim-style indexing.
Typo-resistant fuzzy file search (@) and grep (#).
Interactive TUI with streaming and tool execution.
JSONL session tree (id/parentId) for branching history.
Support for OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google GenAI via Bun's fetch.
Default tools: read, write, edit, bash (sandboxed).
Capability-based extension system for TS skills.
v1_should_have: Git-aware ranking for search (modified/untracked files boosted).
Frecency-based ranking for most used files.
Web-view mode (optional embedding) via Bun's HTTP server.
